name: Hermitages Trail (PRC-BU 120)
name_original: Santibañez del Val desde Quintanilla del Coco — circular por la cascada del Churrión (PRC-BU 120)
description: Hike the moderate 7.7-mile Santibañez del Val circular trail to see the Churrión Cascade, historic hermitages, and juniper forests.
region_intro: Hiking around Quintanilla Del Coco offers routes through varied landscapes, including gorges and hills. The region features natural areas like the Mataviejas Gorge and trails leading to historical hermitages. This terrain provides a mix of easy and moderate paths suitable for different hiking preferences. Best hiking trails around Quintanilla Del Coco The most popular hiking route is Mataviejas Gorge Trail, a 3.5 miles (5.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 28 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the natural contours of the gorge. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mataviejas Gorge Loop Trail, a moderate 6.4 miles (10.4 km) path. This loop offers views of the gorge and surrounding natural areas, typically completed in about 2 hours 58 minutes. Local hikers also love the Hermitages Trail (PRC-BU 120), a 7.7 miles (12.4 km) trail leading through historical sites and natural landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 18 minutes. Hiking around Quintanilla Del Coco is defined by gorges, hills, and cultural sites. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Quintanilla Del Coco are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Quintanilla Del Coco's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Quintanilla Del Coco?
A: Quintanilla Del Coco offers a diverse network of over 45 hiking trails. These routes traverse varied landscapes, including scenic gorges and rolling hills, providing options for different preferences and skill levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Quintanilla Del Coco?
A: Yes, Quintanilla Del Coco has several easy hiking options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1281815249/mataviejas-gorge-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mataviejas Gorge Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.5-mile (5.6 km) route that follows the natural contours of the gorge, perfect for a relaxed walk.

Q: What kind of circular walks can I find in the area?
A: You can find several circular routes that allow you to start and end in the same location. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282042421/mataviejas-gorge-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mataviejas Gorge Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.4-mile (10.4 km) path offering continuous views of the gorge and surrounding natural areas.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking routes in Quintanilla Del Coco?
A: Many of the easier trails are suitable for families. The region's natural areas and relatively gentle terrain on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1281815249/mataviejas-gorge-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mataviejas Gorge Trail&lt;/a&gt; make for enjoyable outings with children, offering accessible paths through picturesque landscapes.

Q: What are some interesting historical or natural landmarks to see while hiking?
A: The trails around Quintanilla Del Coco lead to various points of interest. You can explore historical sites along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1281842601/hermitages-trail-prc-bu-120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitages Trail (PRC-BU 120)&lt;/a&gt;, which passes through natural landscapes and cultural landmarks. Additionally, the region features highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2891679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sad Hill Cemetery&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2521846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Santo Domingo de Silos&lt;/a&gt;, which can be incorporated into your hiking plans.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Quintanilla Del Coco?
A: The best time for hiking in Quintanilla Del Coco is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, while winter offers a different, often quieter, experience, though some paths might be more challenging due to conditions.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty trails for more experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for those seeking a bit more challenge, there are numerous moderate trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292732959/el-montecillo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Montecillo Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5-mile (8.1 km) route that offers varied terrain and elevation changes, providing a rewarding experience for hikers with some experience.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Quintanilla Del Coco?
A: The hiking routes in Quintanilla Del Coco are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the well-maintained paths, and the opportunity to explore both natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Are there any trails that combine natural beauty with cultural sites?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1281842601/hermitages-trail-prc-bu-120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitages Trail (PRC-BU 120)&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent example, leading through natural landscapes while connecting various historical hermitages. This 7.7-mile (12.4 km) trail offers a rich blend of outdoor activity and cultural exploration.

Q: Can I find trails that offer views of the wider region?
A: Many trails in Quintanilla Del Coco provide expansive views, particularly those that ascend the local hills. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1292751880/puentedura-and-mataviejas-gorge-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puentedura and Mataviejas Gorge Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.9-mile (12.7 km) route, offers elevated perspectives of the Mataviejas Gorge and the surrounding countryside.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or summits accessible by hiking?
A: Yes, the region features several points offering panoramic views. Hikers can reach summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5283553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Cabeza Summit&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423526" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro San Cristóbal&lt;/a&gt;, which provide excellent vantage points over the landscape. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4997641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Peña de Carazo&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight for scenic vistas.
tips: The small waterfall of El Churrión, which depending on the time of year can carry a lot of water or be completely dry. The walk is nice and easy. If you are lucky you will see deer in the area.
Small religious temple built at the end of the 9th or beginning of the 10th century. The hermitage has a single nave topped by a flat head made of ashlar stone and sillarejo.
Small cluster of houses located to the west of the town of Santo Domingo de Silos and where the Iglesia de San Juan Bautista is located.

faqs.question: Where is the best place to park for the Santibáñez del Val circular hike?
faqs.answer: The most common starting point for this circular route is the village of Quintanilla del Coco. You can usually find parking within the village, especially near the Church of San Miguel, which is a good reference point to begin your hike.

faqs.question: Is the Santibáñez del Val from Quintanilla del Coco trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally considered of moderate difficulty. While it's described as 'simple without significant elevation changes,' its length of approximately 12 kilometers (7.5 miles) contributes to its moderate rating. It's suitable for hikers with a reasonable level of fitness, but beginners should be prepared for the distance.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this route is during periods of rain to fully appreciate the Churrión Waterfall, which only flows significantly after rainfall. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for hiking in the Burgos province, avoiding the heat of summer and potential snow in winter.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through natural areas and potentially near livestock or wildlife, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail or the natural park?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to hike the PRC-BU 120 trail or to access the Sabinares del Arlanza-La Yecla Natural Park. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of natural beauty and historical interest. Key highlights include the Churrión Waterfall, the Mataviejas Gorge, and diverse landscapes of juniper forests and cultivated fields. You'll also encounter several Romanesque hermitages, such as the Ermita de las Naves and the Hermitage of Santa Cecilia, giving it the alternative name 'Sendero de las Ermitas'. Keep an eye out for a large colony of vultures near the cascade.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the PRC-BU 120 trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, consisting mainly of forest tracks, often through juniper (sabinares) woods, and sections crossing cultivated fields. While there are no major ascents or descents, hikers should be prepared for varying ground conditions, which can be uneven or muddy after rain.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly shares sections with other paths. You'll find it intersecting with parts of 'El Destierro', 'Camino de Santiago de la Lana.BTT', and 'Camino de Santiago de la Lana.Senderista' for short stretches.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or picnic areas along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through the villages of Quintanilla del Coco and Santibáñez del Val, where you might find local establishments. Notably, the area around the Ermita de las Naves offers a picnic area with benches and tables, perfect for a break.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's length and varied terrain, it's advisable to bring sturdy hiking shoes, sufficient water, snacks, and appropriate clothing for the weather conditions. A camera is recommended for the scenic views and historical sites. If hiking during rainy periods, waterproof gear would be beneficial.

faqs.question: Are there any specific natural features or wildlife to look out for?
faqs.answer: Beyond the Churrión Waterfall and the unique juniper forests of the Sabinares del Arlanza, you can often spot a large colony of vultures nesting in the rocky areas near the cascade. The Mataviejas river valley also offers interesting natural landscapes.

waypoints.name: Mozarabic Hermitage of San Juan Bautista (12th Century)
